text: The surgeon evaluates the anatomy, identifying the base of the appendix and tracing it to the tail, as part of the initial steps of a laparoscopic appendectomy.
The surgeon has freed the retrocecal appendix from surrounding structures, revealing its anatomical position and allowing for further manipulation.

The surgeon elevates the freed tail of the appendix into the surgical field and creates a tunnel between the appendix and the mesoappendix, providing access to the mesoappendix and the appendiceal artery.
The surgeon uses a fenestrated bipolar dissector to coagulate the tissue around the appendix, followed by transection with a monopolar energy source from curved scissors.
The surgeon continues the stepwise dissection, progressively freeing the appendix from the mesoappendix while controlling the blood supply.

The tail of the appendix is elevated and freed from surrounding structures. An assistant passes a zero silk suture into the surgical field, and the surgeon ties three simple interrupted sutures around the base of the appendix.

The surgeon uses electrocautery and sharp dissection to transect the base of the appendix, leaving the appendiceal stump visible. A purse string suture is then created around the stump to invert it within the cecum.
